content.full_text:
October 21, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion

Dear Messrs. and Mesdames:

Please accept this as a request for written advice of the Commission pursuant to Government Code §83114 (b) regarding the following factual scenario.

The Monterey City Council is composed of five members. The City is considering adoption of a plan line along a one and one-half mile stretch of Del Monte Avenue within the City which would establish a recorded plan for the ultimate widening of the street. The plan is proposed as a singular project, although it has historically been discussed as having three segments, East, West, and center or Naval Postgraduate School. Although no construction plans for the actual widening of the street are pending, design of a portion is contemplated, and there is no argument that it does establish the City's intent to acquire certain property necessary for the improvements and, as such, could materially impact property values within the plan line.

Two of the Councilmembers, Don Edgren and Carl Outzen, have economic interests in property adjacent to or within the plan line. One, together with his spouse and other partners, owns a visitor accommodation facility in the West segment, the other retains an ownership interest in a business in the East segment. Although segmented, it is important to consider the plan line as a singular element to ensure proper road alignments between segments, and because of funding constraints, it may be necessary to develop one or two of the segments in separate phases, and the decision of which portion to develop first is an integral part of the pending decision.

The Councilmembers involved concede that they have a material financial interest necessitating disqualification from participation in the plan line decisions within 300 feet of their property interests, but have requested that I seek your opinion with respect to the following questions:

1. If the economic effect will be less than $10,000, can they vote on segments of the plan line beyond 300 feet from their property interests?

2. Is it allowable to segment the project and vote on those segments not resulting in a foreseeable material financial conflict?

A portion of the staff report for the project is included to assist in your understanding of the factual basis for the question. Please feel free to contact me at (408) 646-3915 if you have any questions or need any further explanation of the factual scenario. Thanks in advance for your anticipated courtesy and cooperation.

Very truly yours,

William B. Conners
City Attorney

WBC: aof
Enclosure
c: Mayor and Council
    City Manager
    City Clerk
    Community Development Director
    Public Works Director
